q26.s7.next: Retrieve the Golden Order Principia in Leyndell - from the Erdtree Sanctuary, climb the great branch outside to the upper level; the tome rests on a suspended chair. Give it to Corhyn.
q26.s7.hint: A fundamental tome rests high in the Erdtree Sanctuary.
q26.s8.title: Radagon is Marika
q26.s8.recap: You revealed to Goldmask that Radagon and Marika are the same person.
q26.s8.next: Learn Law of Regression from Corhyn (it demands 37 Intelligence) and cast it before the great statue of Radagon in Leyndell, where a message reads 'Regression alone reveals secrets'. Then bring the truth - Radagon is Marika - to Goldmask and Corhyn.
q26.s8.hint: A capital statue and a prayer of regression are tied to Goldmask's riddle.
q26.s8.warn: Complete this revelation before Maliketh falls and the capital turns to ash, or Goldmask's enlightenment never comes.
q26.s9.title: Corhyn's Doubt
q26.s9.recap: Corhyn is troubled by Goldmask's revelations.
q26.s9.next: Find Corhyn and Goldmask on the bridge south of Stargazers' Ruins, above the Ancient Snow Valley Ruins grace in the Mountaintops of the Giants; Corhyn's faith wavers under the weight of the truth. (Leyndell's Ashen Capital is where their story concludes, later.)
q26.s9.hint: Faith and doubt sit side by side on a bridge south of Stargazers' Ruins, in the Mountaintops of the Giants.
q26.s10.title: The Perfect Order
q26.s10.recap: You found the Mending Rune of Perfect Order on Goldmask's body.
q26.s10.next: Return once more: Goldmask has passed. Take the Mending Rune of Perfect Order from his body - it enables the Age of Order ending.
q26.s10.hint: Goldmask's contemplation has reached its end in the capital.
q26.s10.warn: Finish the Radagon revelation before burning the Erdtree region into ash, or Goldmask disappears and Corhyn's faith is lost.
q26.s99.title: Corhyn's Death
q27.name: Latenna's Quest
q27.start: Slumbering Wolf's Shack, behind the Lakeside Crystal Cave (south Liurnia) — Albus's medallion half in hand
q27.s1.title: Albus' Medallion
q27.s1.recap: You found Albus and received half of the Haligtree medallion.
q27.s1.next: In the Village of the Albinaurics (Liurnia), strike the lone pot on the ledge to reveal Albus; he entrusts you the RIGHT half of the Haligtree Secret Medallion.
q27.s1.hint: In the ruined village of the silver folk, one pot sits strangely out of place.
q27.s2.title: Latenna's Request
q27.s2.recap: You showed the medallion to Latenna; she asked for your help.
q27.s2.next: Pass through the Lakeside Crystal Cave (past its Bloodhound Knight) to the Slumbering Wolf's Shack and show Latenna the medallion half; she asks to be taken to the Haligtree and joins you as a spirit ash.
q27.s2.hint: An archer and her wolf shelter in a shack beyond the crystal cave.
q27.s3.title: The Haligtree Path
q27.s3.recap: After reaching the Consecrated Snowfield, Latenna pointed you to the Haligtree.
q27.s3.next: Take the LEFT medallion half from Castle Sol in the northern Mountaintops (defeat Commander Niall), then use both halves at the Grand Lift of Rold to reach the Consecrated Snowfield; Latenna speaks of the Haligtree ahead.
q27.s3.hint: The medallion's other half lies with the commander of a fortress of the eclipse.
q27.s3.warn: Meet Latenna and take her ashes before fetching the second half - travellers report her tale can stall otherwise.
q27.s4.title: Latenna's Final Rest
q27.s4.recap: Latenna reached her journey's end at the Apostate Derelict, leaving you a Somber Ancient Dragon Smithing Stone.
q27.s4.next: At the Apostate Derelict in the Consecrated Snowfield's far northwest, summon Latenna's ashes before the giant Albinauric woman; her journey ends, and she leaves you a Somber Ancient Dragon Smithing Stone.
q27.s4.hint: At the snowfield's far northwest, a giant of Latenna's kind waits in a derelict church.
q27.s99.title: Latenna's Death
q28.name: Bernahl's Quest
q28.s1.title: Bernahl's Lessons
q28.s1.recap: You met Bernahl at the Warmaster's Shack; he teaches Ashes of War.
q28.s1.next: Meet Knight Bernahl at the Warmaster's Shack on Stormhill; he sells Ashes of War.
q28.s1.hint: A war master idles at a shack on Stormhill, selling the arts of battle.
q28.s2.title: Volcano Manor Warning
q28.s2.recap: You met Bernahl at Volcano Manor; he warned you about the nature of the Manor.
q28.s2.next: After joining Volcano Manor, find Bernahl in the drawing room; he speaks plainly about what the Manor is.
q28.s2.hint: The war master has taken up an old cause at Volcano Manor.
q28.s3.title: First Mission Reward
q28.s3.recap: Bernahl started teaching new arts after you completed your first Manor mission.
q28.s3.next: Complete the Manor's first assassination request, then speak with Bernahl; he acknowledges you as a fellow recusant, his wares still on offer.
q28.s3.hint: After the Manor's first contract, Bernahl may have more to say.
q28.s4.title: The Roundtable Hunt
q28.s4.recap: Bernahl gave you the Letter to Bernahl, naming other Tarnished of the Roundtable to hunt.
q28.s4.next: Receive the Letter to Bernahl: two Tarnished of the Roundtable - Vargram the Raging Wolf and Errant Sorcerer Wilhelm - are to be hunted.
q28.s4.hint: Bernahl has a letter of his own for you.
q28.s5.title: Hunt Complete
q28.s5.recap: You completed the hunt, slaying Vargram and Wilhelm.
q28.s5.next: Invade via the red summon sign in Leyndell, Royal Capital, and slay Vargram and Wilhelm together.
q28.s5.hint: Your marks were last seen inside the royal capital.
q28.s6.title: Bernahl's Reward
q28.s6.recap: You reported back to Bernahl and received your reward.
q28.s6.next: Report back to Bernahl at Volcano Manor for your reward.
q28.s6.hint: Bernahl will want word of the hunt.
q28.s7.title: Rising Against the Erdtree
q28.s7.recap: After killing Rykard, Bernahl mentioned rising against the Erdtree.
q28.s7.next: After Rykard is slain, speak with Bernahl at the Manor; he takes up his old cause once more - to rise against the Erdtree - and departs.
q28.s7.hint: With the Manor's lord fallen, Bernahl speaks of his own war.
q28.s8.title: The Farum Azula Duel
q28.s8.recap: You defeated Recusant Bernahl in Farum Azula, claiming the Blasphemous Claw and his scepter.
q28.s8.next: From the Beside the Great Bridge grace in Crumbling Farum Azula, go through the door and up the stairs, then north toward the dragon temple and on to the eastern lone tower near the Old Lord's Talisman chest - Recusant Bernahl invades there. Defeat him for the Blasphemous Claw - keep it: it can parry Maliketh's black blade - and his Devourer's Scepter.
q28.s8.hint: Past the Great Bridge, an apostate knight watches the path toward an old talisman.
q28.s99.title: Bernahl's Death
q29.name: The Purpose of a God
q29.start: Gravesite Plain, near the Realm of Shadow's first graces
q29.s1.title: Miquella's Footprints
q29.s1.recap: You met Freyja in the Realm of Shadow; she fought alongside Radahn.
q29.s1.next: Speak with Redmane Freyja by her campfire at the Three-Path Cross on the Gravesite Plain; once one of Radahn's warriors, she now follows Miquella.
q29.s1.hint: A Redmane warrior warms herself by a campfire at the Three-Path Cross.
q29.s2.title: In Search of Purpose
q29.s2.recap: You met Sir Ansbach, a former follower of Mohg, investigating Miquella's purpose.
q29.s2.next: Meet Sir Ansbach at the Main Gate Cross before Belurat (beside Moore); once Mohg's right hand, he quietly probes Miquella's purpose.
q29.s2.hint: An old knight of the Lord of Blood watches Belurat's approach.
q29.s3.title: The Nature of the Vow
q29.s3.recap: After the Great Rune shattered, Freyja departed to understand Miquella's vow to Radahn.
q29.s3.next: When you reach the Shadow Keep's gates, a great rune shatters and the charm binding the company frays. Exhaust Freyja's dialogue back at the Three-Path Cross on the Gravesite Plain; she resolves to learn the nature of Miquella's vow to Radahn, then relocates to the Specimen Storehouse, Seventh Floor.
q29.s3.hint: After the great rune shatters, Freyja returns to the Three-Path Cross with questions.
q29.s4.title: A Pure Monster
q29.s4.recap: Ansbach told you about his challenge to Miquella after the shattering.
q29.s4.next: Speak with Ansbach after the rune shatters; he tells you of the challenge he once made to Miquella, and moves on.
q29.s4.hint: The old knight may read the shattered rune differently.
q29.s5.title: Body Snatching
q29.s5.recap: Ansbach pondered Mohg's body being brought to Miquella.
q29.s5.next: Find Ansbach on the first floor of the Specimen Storehouse in the Shadow Keep, troubled by word that Mohg's body was carried into this land.
q29.s5.hint: The old knight has taken to the storehouse's books.
q29.s6.title: Hard Road Ahead
q29.s6.recap: You informed Ansbach about Freyja's decision.
q29.s6.next: Tell Ansbach of Freyja's decision to seek the meaning of Miquella's vow.
q29.s6.hint: Freyja's decision may matter to Ansbach.
q29.s7.title: Is That Even a Word?
q29.s7.recap: You found Freyja trying to read a stone slab.
q29.s7.next: Find Freyja high in the Specimen Storehouse, puzzling over a stone slab she cannot read, and speak with her.
q29.s7.hint: Somewhere high in the storehouse, Freyja squints at old stone.
q29.s8.title: The Secret Rite Scroll
q29.s8.recap: You retrieved a Secret Rite Scroll, its purpose unknown.
q29.s8.next: Retrieve the Secret Rite Scroll from the Specimen Storehouse, Fourth Floor: take the stairway down just before the bats, and find it on a table behind a bookcase.
q29.s8.hint: Search the fourth floor of the Specimen Storehouse for a hidden rite.
q29.s9.title: Used and Abused
q29.s9.recap: Ansbach discovered Miquella intends to use Mohg's corpse as a vessel for his consort.
q29.s9.next: Give the Secret Rite Scroll to Ansbach; he discerns Miquella's design - Mohg's corpse is to serve as a vessel.
q29.s9.hint: The old knight should see what the storehouse kept hidden.
q29.s10.title: Stoking the Flames of War
q29.s10.recap: Ansbach gave you a letter for Freyja to give her the answers she seeks.
q29.s10.next: Carry Ansbach's letter to Freyja; it holds the answers she has been seeking.
q29.s10.hint: Ansbach's letter is meant for Freyja.
q29.s11.title: The Great Lion, Reborn
q29.s11.recap: Freyja read that Miquella wants to revive General Radahn.
q29.s11.next: Speak with Freyja as she reads the letter: Miquella means to revive General Radahn. Her course is set - she will fight for her lord once more.
q29.s11.hint: Freyja needs time with Ansbach's letter.
q29.s12.title: The Redmane's Message
q29.s12.recap: You reported Freyja's words back to Ansbach.
q29.s12.next: Report Freyja's response to Ansbach; the old knight readies himself for what must come.
q29.s12.hint: Ansbach will want to hear Freyja's answer.
q29.s13.title: To Challenge a God
q29.s13.recap: You supported Ansbach until the end. Only one last foe remains.
q29.s13.next: In Enir-Ilim, stand with Ansbach: he aids you against Leda's ambush, and his gold summon sign can join you against the final foe. Freyja, true to her lord, may stand among your enemies there.
q29.s13.hint: The old knight's cause leads toward the tower's summit.
q29.s13.warn: Complete Ansbach's steps before the final battles of Enir-Ilim, or his aid (and his story's end) is lost.
q31.name: Revenge
q31.start: Three-Path Cross, Gravesite Plain (Realm of Shadow)
q31.s1.title: In Pursuit of Revenge
q31.s1.recap: Hornsent seeks vengeance for his clan.
q31.s1.next: Speak with the Hornsent at the Three-Path Cross on the Gravesite Plain; he gives you the Cross Map tracing Miquella's path, and seeks revenge for his slaughtered people. (Gift him a Scorpion Stew and he shares Furnace Visages.)
q31.s1.hint: A horned stranger at the Three-Path Cross tracks Miquella's footsteps.
q31.s2.title: Not Worthy
q31.s2.recap: You told Leda that Hornsent is unworthy of being at Miquella's side.
q31.s2.next: After the great rune shatters, speak with Needle Knight Leda at the Highroad Cross about the Hornsent; freed of Miquella's charm, she deems him a danger to the company.
q31.s2.hint: Leda has begun to question the horned one.
q31.s3.title: Eliminating Dissent
q31.s3.recap: Leda wants to eliminate Hornsent, fearing he will turn on Miquella.
q31.s3.next: Leda moves to eliminate the Hornsent: summon signs appear near the Shadow Keep - aid Leda in her ambush, or answer the Hornsent's sign and defend him.
q31.s3.hint: Near Shadow Keep, Leda and the horned one are drawing steel.
q31.s3.warn: Branch point: siding with Leda kills the Hornsent (his story ends); defending him keeps his revenge - and his aid against Messmer - alive.
q31.s4.title: Putting Down The Serpent
q31.s4.recap: You summoned Hornsent to fight Messmer the Impaler.
q31.s4.next: Summon the Hornsent for the fight against Messmer the Impaler in the Shadow Keep's Dark Chamber; his vengeance burns hottest there.
q31.s4.hint: The Hornsent's vengeance points toward the serpent atop the keep.
q31.s5.title: Unquenchable Thirst
q31.s5.recap: Messmer's death did not sate the Hornsent; his crusade against Marika's kind turned upon you near the Church of the Bud.
q31.s5.next: If you summoned the Hornsent against Messmer and exhausted his dialogue afterward, he invades you at the top of the staircase near the Church of the Bud, Main Entrance, in the Ancient Ruins of Rauh - his crusade against Marika's kind turns on you at last. (If you never triggered that invasion, he instead appears as a hostile NPC during the final battle in Enir-Ilim.)
q31.s5.hint: After the serpent falls, the horned one's trail leads toward the Church of the Bud.
q32.name: Fighting for a Faraway Light
q32.start: A prayer echoes from a cell in the Belurat Gaol (Realm of Shadow)
q32.s1.title: The Fire Knight's Invasions
q32.s1.recap: You defeated Fire Knight Queelign's two invasions and found his physical body in Shadow Keep.
q32.s1.next: Fire Knight Queelign invades you twice: first at Belurat Tower Settlement, in the circular courtyard near the Small Private Altar grace - defeating him drops the Crusade Insignia talisman - then at the Church of the Crusade in Scadu Altus, where defeating him drops the Prayer Room Key and Ash of War: Flame Skewer. Use the key to reach his physical body in the Prayer Room of Shadow Keep's Church District.
q32.s1.hint: A fire knight's phantom haunts Belurat and the Church of the Crusade before his trail leads to Shadow Keep's Church District.
q32.s2.title: Spirit Renewed
q32.s2.recap: You used the Iris of Grace on Queelign; he became your spirit ash.
q32.s2.next: Give Queelign an Iris of Grace in the Prayer Room, Shadow Keep's Church District: his faith rekindled, he offers himself as a spirit ash.
q32.s2.hint: In the Prayer Room, the fire knight waits for an iris.
q32.s2.warn: Branch point: the Iris of Grace redeems him; the Iris of Occultation destroys him.
q32.s3.title: Why Have You Forsaken Me?
q32.s3.recap: You used the Iris of Occultation on Queelign, denying him all light and leaving him in despair.
q32.s3.next: Alternatively, give Queelign an Iris of Occultation in the Prayer Room, Shadow Keep's Church District: denied even his distant light, he dies in despair, dropping Queelign's Greatsword.
q32.s3.hint: In the Prayer Room, the fire knight waits for an iris.
q32.s3.warn: Branch point: the Iris of Grace redeems him; the Iris of Occultation destroys him.
q33.name: Star-Crossed
q33.start: Cathedral of Manus Metyr (Realm of Shadow)
q33.s1.title: Chance Encounter
q33.s1.recap: You met Ymir at the Cathedral of Manus Metyr; he gave you a map to old ruins.
q33.s1.next: Meet Count Ymir at the Cathedral of Manus Metyr in eastern Scadu Altus (beyond Bonny Village); he sells sorceries, keeps the swordhand Jolán at his side, and gives you a map to the Finger Ruins of Rhia.
q33.s1.hint: A high priest keeps a cathedral of the Fingers, east beyond Bonny Village.
q33.s2.title: A Fingery Bell
q33.s2.recap: You rang the bell in the Finger Ruins of Rhia.
q33.s2.next: Travel to the Finger Ruins of Rhia on the eastern Cerulean Coast and ring the Fingerruin bell.
q33.s2.hint: A great bell waits among finger-shaped ruins on the southern coast.
q33.s3.title: Guiding Stars
q33.s3.recap: Ymir gave you a new map to the Finger Ruins of Dheo.
q33.s3.next: Return to Ymir; pleased, he gives you a second map - to the Finger Ruins of Dheo.
q33.s3.hint: The count has another map for a diligent bell-ringer.
q33.s4.title: Second Round
q33.s4.recap: You rang the bell in the Finger Ruins of Dheo.
q33.s4.next: From the Shadow Keep, Back Gate site of grace, find the altar in the room to the left and perform the 'O Mother' gesture before it (found north of Bonny Village) to open a hidden tunnel into the Hinterlands. Cross to the Finger Ruins of Dheo and ring the second Fingerruin bell.
q33.s4.hint: The second bell lies beyond Shadow Keep's Back Gate, somewhere in the hidden Hinterlands.
q33.s5.title: Joining The Stars
q33.s5.recap: Ymir gave you a final map after you rang both bells.
q33.s5.next: Return to Count Ymir once more; he speaks of a final site - and of the throne on which he sits.
q33.s5.hint: Ymir's next secret is still at the cathedral.
q33.s6.title: Mother of Fingers
q33.s6.recap: Beneath the cathedral you rang the last bell, felled Metyr, and put down Count Ymir on his throne.
q33.s6.next: Open the passage beneath Ymir's throne and descend to the Finger Ruins of Miyr; ring the last bell to face Metyr, Mother of Fingers. Return to the cathedral afterward: Count Ymir, Mother of Fingers, awaits - defeat him (Jolán's blade joins his).
q33.s6.hint: The count's chair hides more than it should.
q33.s7.title: A Night That Never Was
q33.s7.recap: You gave the Iris of Grace to Jolan, obtaining her Spirit Ash.
q33.s7.next: Give the dying Jolán an Iris of Grace by the cathedral: her night restored, she offers her spirit ash in service.
q33.s7.hint: The swordhand of night lies by the cathedral, and an iris may answer her.
q33.s7.warn: Branch point: one iris for her service, the other for her rest.
q33.s8.title: Our Night
q33.s8.recap: You gave the Iris of Occultation to Jolan, granting her repose.
q33.s8.next: Alternatively, give Jolán an Iris of Occultation, granting her the repose she longs for.
q33.s8.hint: The swordhand of night lies by the cathedral, and an iris may answer her.
q33.s8.warn: Branch point: one iris for her service, the other for her rest.
q34.name: The Oldest And Vilest
q34.start: Pillar Path Waypoint, south of the Gravesite Plain (Realm of Shadow)
q34.s1.title: Too Angry To Die
q34.s1.recap: You met Igon, a wounded man yelling at the Pillar Path Waypoint.
q34.s1.next: Meet Igon - the wounded man howling curses - at the Pillar Path Waypoint, south of the Gravesite Plain, and hear out his hatred of Bayle the Dread.
q34.s1.hint: A broken voice curses a dragon's name from the roadside south of the plain.
q34.s2.title: Traitor To Dragons
q34.s2.recap: The Dragon Communion Priestess told you to slay Bayle and devour its heart.
q34.s2.next: Speak with the Dragon Communion Priestess at the Grand Altar of Dragon Communion (west, through the Dragon's Pit); she names Bayle traitor to dragonkind and bids you slay him and devour his heart.
q34.s2.hint: The Grand Altar of Dragon Communion knows the name Igon curses.
q34.s3.title: A Warrior Until The End
q34.s3.recap: Igon asked you to summon him when you face Bayle.
q34.s3.next: Climb the Jagged Peak (past the duelling dragons); Igon lies partway up and presses Igon's Furled Finger on you - vow to summon him against Bayle.
q34.s3.hint: Igon's hatred points up the Jagged Peak.
q34.s4.title: CURSE YOU, BAYLE!
q34.s4.recap: With Igon at your side, Bayle the Dread was struck down; the roaring hunter found his peace.
q34.s4.next: At the peak's summit, summon Igon by his sign and destroy Bayle the Dread. His vengeance spent, Igon rests at last - his effects remain where he lay, and the Priestess honors Bayle's heart at the Grand Altar.
q34.s4.hint: The summit of Jagged Peak is where Igon's curse is aimed.
q35.name: Deep, Cursed Slumber
q35.start: Pillar Path Cross, Gravesite Plain — Moore's black syrup opens the way
q35.s1.title: Express Delivery
q35.s1.recap: Thiollier gave you his concoction after receiving Moore's item.
q35.s1.next: Deliver Moore's black syrup to Thiollier at the Pillar Path Cross; in thanks, he mixes you Thiollier's Concoction.
q35.s1.hint: A soft-spoken poisoner idles at the Pillar Path Cross; Moore has a gift for him.
q35.s2.title: Dreadful Sleep
q35.s2.recap: You found a sleeping flower creature (St. Trina) in a deep cave on the Southern Coast.
q35.s2.next: Descend the Stone Coffin Fissure south of the Cerulean Coast and defeat the Putrescent Knight; in the Garden of Deep Purple beyond sleeps St. Trina. Drink her nectar - the death it brings is part of its truth.
q35.s2.hint: Deep violet lilies are said to bloom past the fissure at the southern coast.
q35.s3.title: Longing For Sleep
q35.s3.recap: You told Thiollier about St. Trina's whereabouts; he ventured there.
q35.s3.next: Tell Thiollier where St. Trina lies; he sets out for the fissure at once. (If Moore has already told him of the southern lilies, his summon sign can even join you against the Putrescent Knight.)
q35.s3.hint: Thiollier is searching for where St. Trina withers.
q35.s4.title: Sweet Poison
q35.s4.recap: You found Thiollier in St. Trina's cave, lost in eternal slumber.
q35.s4.next: Find Thiollier drowsing beside St. Trina in the Garden of Deep Purple, lost in the nectar's sleep.
q35.s4.hint: The Garden of Deep Purple is worth revisiting once Thiollier follows the lilies.
q35.s5.title: Poor Thing
q35.s5.recap: St. Trina spoke to you after you drank her nectar enough times.
q35.s5.next: Drink St. Trina's nectar again and again (each drink a small death - three more at least) until her voice reaches you in the dark.
q35.s5.hint: St. Trina's nectar asks for more than one taste.
q35.s5.warn: You must drink several times, dying each time, before St. Trina speaks.
q35.s6.title: Don't Lie to Me
q35.s6.recap: You told Thiollier about St. Trina's words; he didn't believe you.
q35.s6.next: Tell Thiollier what St. Trina said; he refuses to believe that YOU heard her voice.
q35.s6.hint: Thiollier should hear what you learned in the garden.
q35.s7.title: Don't Mock Me
q35.s7.recap: Thiollier refuses to hear your words.
q35.s7.next: Press him - or drink once more - and Thiollier's envy boils over; he turns hostile.
q35.s7.hint: Thiollier's doubt may not be settled yet.
q35.s8.title: Mine, And Mine Alone
q35.s8.recap: You defeated Thiollier after he attacked you in anger.
q35.s8.next: Rest or drink the nectar again to trigger Thiollier's hostile phantom in the Garden of Deep Purple (St. Trina's room); defeat him there - he drops St. Trina's Smile - and speak to him again in the garden, humbled.
q35.s8.hint: The Garden of Deep Purple is where Thiollier's challenge takes shape.
q35.s9.title: Nightmare
q35.s9.recap: After defeating his hostile phantom and drinking the nectar twice more, you brought Thiollier St. Trina's words - that Miquella must die.
q35.s9.next: After defeating Thiollier's hostile phantom, drink St. Trina's nectar twice more; she bids you kill Miquella. Bring her words to Thiollier before facing Leda's ambush in Enir-Ilim - at last, he hears them and believes you.
q35.s9.hint: After Thiollier's challenge, return to St. Trina's nectar before leaving for Enir-Ilim.
q35.s9.warn: Deliver Trina's words before facing Leda's ambush in Enir-Ilim, or Thiollier disappears for good (Moore inherits his wares). Waiting too long after defeating his phantom can also end his tale early.
q35.s10.title: Her Voice
q35.s10.recap: Thiollier stood with you at Enir-Ilim, St. Trina's will his own at last.
q35.s10.next: At Enir-Ilim's Cleansing Chamber Anteroom, summon Thiollier's gold sign before Leda's ambush - he fights beside you, and his sign can join the final battle too. Whatever peace he finds, he returns to Trina's side after.
q35.s10.hint: Thiollier's path now leads toward the tower.
q36.name: Blackguard Big Boggart's Quest
q36.start: Boilprawn Shack, southern Liurnia (near the Grand Lift of Dectus)
q36.s1.title: The Necklace Thief
q36.s1.recap: You bought back Rya's stolen necklace from Blackguard Big Boggart at the Boilprawn Shack.
q36.s1.next: Speak with Rya near the Laskyar Ruins, then buy her stolen necklace back from Blackguard Big Boggart at the Boilprawn Shack for 1,000 runes.
q36.s1.hint: A blackguard camped by a prawn shack in southern Liurnia is holding onto something that isn't his.
q36.s1.warn: Killing Boggart here instead of paying still yields the necklace, but ends his own story early and forfeits everything below.
q36.s2.title: A Taste for Prawns
q36.s2.recap: You bought Boggart's boiled prawns; trusting you, he decided to relocate toward Leyndell.
q36.s2.next: Buy Boiled Prawns from Boggart at the Boilprawn Shack. Trusting you now, he packs up to relocate toward Leyndell's outer moat.
q36.s2.hint: A rough man may soften if you show an interest in what he has to offer.
q36.s3.title: Warnings at the Capital Rampart
q36.s3.recap: Boggart relocated to Leyndell's outer moat, selling Boiled Crabs and warning you about the Dung Eater.
q36.s3.next: Find Boggart near the Capital Rampart, in Leyndell's outer moat; he now sells Boiled Crabs and speaks of horrors he's witnessed the Dung Eater commit.
q36.s3.hint: Boggart has moved on to the capital's outer walls.
q36.s3.warn: Skip meeting him here and he survives the Dung Eater's later invasion (no death-loot, but he lives); confirm this meeting and he is killed when the Dung Eater strikes.
q36.s4.title: Milos's Sword
q36.s4.recap: The Dung Eater's phantom invaded near the outer moat; you defeated it and claimed Milos's Sword.
q36.s4.next: After freeing the Dung Eater's body from the Shunning-Grounds and resting, his phantom invades near Leyndell's outer moat -- defeat it for Milos's Sword.
q36.s4.hint: Something is stirring near the outer moat, tied to the Dung Eater's own fate.
q36.s99.title: Blackguard's End
q36.s99.next: Boggart is dead -- if he was met at the Capital Rampart, loot his body for the Seedbed Curse, Iron Ball, Blackguard's Iron Mask, and Blackguard's Bell Bearing.
q36.s99.warn: Killing him yourself in Liurnia still lets you buy the necklace first, but forfeits his later stock and death-loot.
q37.name: Tanith's Quest
q37.start: Volcano Manor, Altus Plateau (reached via Rya's invitation)
q37.s1.title: Joining the Family
q37.s1.recap: You accepted Tanith's offer and joined the Volcano Manor family, receiving the Drawing-Room Key.
q37.s1.next: Speak with Tanith in Volcano Manor's entrance hall and accept membership; she gives you the Drawing-Room Key.
q37.s1.hint: The mistress of Volcano Manor welcomes new arrivals in its entrance hall.
q37.s2.title: First Order: Old Knight Istvan
q37.s2.recap: You accepted the Manor's first assassination request and defeated Old Knight Istvan near Stormhill's Divine Bridge.
q37.s2.next: Take the first assassination letter from the drawing room table, then invade via the red summon sign near the Divine Bridge on Stormhill (Limgrave) and defeat Old Knight Istvan. Report back to Tanith for the Magma Shot sorcery.
q37.s2.hint: The Manor's first contract points toward a bridge on Stormhill.
q37.s3.title: Second Order: Rileigh the Idle
q37.s3.recap: You accepted the Manor's second assassination request and defeated Rileigh the Idle on the Altus Plateau.
q37.s3.next: Take the second assassination letter from the drawing room, then invade and defeat Rileigh the Idle on the Altus Plateau. Report back to Tanith for the Serpentbone Blade.
q37.s3.hint: The Manor's second contract leads to the Altus Plateau.
q37.s4.title: Third Order: Juno Hoslow
q37.s4.recap: You accepted the Manor's third assassination request and defeated Juno Hoslow, Knight of Blood, in the Mountaintops of the Giants.
q37.s4.next: Take the third assassination letter from the drawing room, then invade and defeat Juno Hoslow, Knight of Blood, in the Mountaintops of the Giants. Report back to Tanith for the Taker's Cameo talisman.
q37.s4.hint: The Manor's third and final contract lies far to the east, among the giants' mountaintops.
q37.s5.title: Defeat Rykard, Lord of Blasphemy
q37.s5.recap: You completed all three assassination requests and defeated Rykard, Lord of Blasphemy.
q37.s5.next: With all three requests complete, accept Tanith's invitation to face the Manor's true lord; defeat Rykard, Lord of Blasphemy, in his boss chamber deep within the Manor.
q37.s5.hint: Completing every contract opens the way to the Manor's hidden master.
q37.s5.warn: Defeating Rykard before finishing all three assassination requests forfeits the remaining letters (and Bernahl's own hunt) for good.
q37.s6.title: The Serpent's Feast
q37.s6.recap: With Rykard slain, Tanith moved to his corpse and revealed her true, serpentine nature, feasting on his remains.
q37.s6.next: Return to Rykard's boss chamber; Tanith has moved to his corpse and now feasts there, revealing her serpentine nature. Her tale ends here.
q37.s6.hint: The Manor's hostess may have gone to pay her respects to its fallen lord.
q37.s6.warn: Attacking Tanith at any point summons Tanith's Knight to defend her; killing her forfeits this scene but drops the Consort's Set, and her knight drops the Aspect of the Crucible: Breath prayer.
